requirements:
Requirement 1: Implement role-based authentication and authorization for at least these roles: Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Administrative Staff, and Student.

Requirement 2: Allow the Administrator to create, edit, and delete teaching modules.

Requirement 3: Allow the Administrator to associate one or more referring teachers with a teaching module and to modify those associations.

Requirement 4: Provide a global user database (teachers and students) managed by the Administrator, supporting create, read, update, and delete operations on user records.

Requirement 5: Allow the Administrator to assign teachers and students to modules (enroll/unenroll users) from the global database.

Requirement 6: Allow a Referring Teacher to add existing teachers and existing students (already present in the global database) to their module(s).

Requirement 7: Enable a Teacher to record an absence for a student in a specific module and to include an optional textual comment with the absence record.

Requirement 8: Record metadata for each absence entry: reporter (user who recorded it), date/time, module, student, comment, and current justification status.

Requirement 9: Allow Teachers to view a list of absences for all students enrolled in any module they teach.

Requirement 10: Allow Administrative Staff to view all student absences across the system and to enter or update the reason and justification status for any absence.

Requirement 11: Support grouping and filtering of absence records by module and by student.

Requirement 12: Support filtering and reporting based on absence justification status (e.g., justified, unjustified, pending).

Requirement 13: Provide an alerts page for teaching staff that lists students who have more than three unjustified absences (threshold configurable by Administrator).

Requirement 1: Provide role-based authentication and authorization for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff accounts.

Requirement 2: Provide a portal dashboard per role that exposes only the functions and data allowed for that role.

Requirement 3: Allow the Administrator to create, modify, and delete teaching modules.

Requirement 4: Allow the Administrator to associate each module with one or more referring teachers and update those associations.

Requirement 5: Maintain a global database of teachers and students that the Administrator can create, update, deactivate, and delete.

Requirement 6: Allow a Referring Teacher to add (assign) existing registered teachers and students from the global database to their modules.

Requirement 7: Allow Teachers to record an absence for a student in a module they teach and to include an optional comment when reporting the absence.

Requirement 8: Record for each absence: student, module, reporting teacher, date/time, and any teacher comment.

Requirement 9: Allow Administrative Staff to enter or update the reason for an absence and to mark an absence as justified or unjustified.

Requirement 10: Grant Administrative Staff read access to all student absence records and the ability to search and filter them.

Requirement 11: Allow Teachers to view the list of absences for all students enrolled in any module they teach, with filtering by module, student, date range, and justification status.

Requirement 12: Support grouping and viewing absence records by module and by student.

Requirement 13: Allow Students to submit a justification request for an absence (including comments and optional supporting evidence) to be reviewed by Administrative Staff.

Requirement 14: Allow Administrative Staff to review student justifications, accept or reject them, and record the decision and rationale on the absence record.

Requirement 15: Provide a page/dashboard that alerts teaching staff to students who have more than three unjustified absences, and ensure the alert list is updated automatically.

Requirement 16: Maintain an audit trail of all absence-related actions (creation, edits, justification submissions, status changes) including user and timestamp.

Requirement 1: The system shall support user authentication and role-based access control for at least the roles: Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Administrative Staff, and Student.

Requirement 2: The Administrator shall be able to create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) teaching modules.

Requirement 3: The Administrator shall be able to assign one or more referring teachers to a module and update or remove those associations.

Requirement 4: The Administrator shall manage a global user database of teachers and students (CRUD on user records) and group users as needed.

Requirement 5: Referring Teachers shall be able to add existing registered teachers and students from the global database to their module(s).

Requirement 6: The system shall allow a teacher to record an absence for a student in a specific module, specifying date/time, absent student, reporting teacher, and allowing an optional comment.

Requirement 7: The system shall record metadata for each absence: module, student, date/time, reporting teacher, comment, and justification status/reason.

Requirement 8: Teachers shall be able to view a list of all absences for every student enrolled in any module they teach.

Requirement 9: Administrative Staff shall be able to view all recorded student absences across modules and students.

Requirement 10: Administrative Staff shall be able to enter or update the official reason/justification for any recorded absence and change the absence justification status.

Requirement 11: The system shall provide views/reports that group and present absences by module and by student.

Requirement 12: The system shall provide a page accessible to teaching staff that lists students who have more than three unjustified absences and highlights these cases.

Requirement 13: Students shall be able to view their own absence records and submit a justification request to Administrative Staff for an absence.

Requirement 14: The system shall maintain an audit trail for absence records and changes (who recorded/edited an absence, who entered justification, and timestamps).

Requirement 15: The system shall prevent adding users to modules unless the users already exist in the global database (referring teachers may only add registered users).

Requirement 1: Support four user roles with authentication and role-based access control: Administrator, Administrative Staff, Teacher, and Student.

Requirement 2: Allow the Administrator to create, update, and delete teaching modules.

Requirement 3: Allow the Administrator to assign one or more referring teachers to each teaching module and change those associations.

Requirement 4: Provide a global user database managed by the Administrator containing teacher and student records, with create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations.

Requirement 5: Allow referring teachers to add (assign/enroll) existing teachers and existing students from the global database to their modules.

Requirement 6: Allow a Teacher to record an absence for a student in a specific module and to attach an optional comment to that absence entry.

Requirement 7: Allow Teachers to view the list of absences for all students enrolled in any module they teach.

Requirement 8: Allow Administrative Staff to view all student absences across the system.

Requirement 9: Allow Administrative Staff to enter or update the reason/status for any recorded absence (e.g., justified, unjustified, pending).

Requirement 10: Store for each absence: student, module, date/time, reporting teacher, comment, administrative reason/status, and modification history.

Requirement 11: Provide views/reports that group and display absences by module and by student.

Requirement 12: Provide a page or view that alerts teaching staff to students who have more than three unjustified absences.

Requirement 13: Allow Students to view their own absence records and current justification/status for each absence.

Requirement 14: Provide search and lookup functionality so users (teachers/administrators) can find existing students and teachers in the global database when assigning them to modules.

Requirement 1: Provide role-based user accounts and authentication for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Administrative Staff, and Student.

Requirement 2: Allow the Administrator to create, update, and delete teaching modules.

Requirement 3: Allow the Administrator to associate one or more referring teachers with each teaching module and to change those associations.

Requirement 4: Maintain a global database of teachers and students that the Administrator can create, update, delete, and view.

Requirement 5: Allow the Administrator to group teachers and students (e.g., cohorts, classes) and manage those groups.

Requirement 6: Allow a Referring Teacher to add existing registered teachers and students to their module(s) (i.e., enroll users in a module) if those users already exist in the global database.

Requirement 7: Allow Teachers to record an absence for a student in a specific module and to attach an optional comment to that absence record.

Requirement 8: Allow Teachers to view a list of absences for all students enrolled in any module for which they are assigned/authorized.

Requirement 9: Allow Administrative Staff to view all student absences across the system and to enter or edit the administrative reason/justification for any absence.

Requirement 10: Track and store the status of each absence (e.g., unjustified, justified) along with teacher comments, administrative reasons, timestamps, and the identity of the user who recorded/modified it.

Requirement 11: Provide views and reporting that group and filter absences by module and by student (with date-range and sorting options).

Requirement 12: Provide a dedicated alert page that lists students with more than three unjustified absences and make that page accessible to teaching staff (and Administrators).

Requirement 13: Restrict data access according to role: teachers may see only absences for students in their modules, administrative staff and administrators may see all absences, and students may view their own absence records and justifications.

Requirement 14: Maintain an audit log of absence-related actions (creation, modification, justification) including user, action, and timestamp.
